meaning: n.分开,拆开,特遣部队,超然,分离,分遣队
examples: She felt a sense of detachment from what was going on.
His mask of detachment cracked, and she saw for an instant an angry and violent man.
There is this "detachment" and I realize that there are obviously experiences which would overwhelm everyone.
Eventually, twelve hundred Jews seeped out of the ghettos and joined the Bielski Otriad (Detachment).
But Obama has a certain emotional detachment that has turned off many of them.
